description: This figure presents experimental and simulated dynamic recrystallization (DRX) microstructure evolution of low-alloy steel under hot compression at 950°C, where strain rate transiently decreases from 0.1 s⁻¹ to 0.01 s⁻¹ at ε=0.4. Subfigure (a) shows experimental EBSD inverse pole figure (IPF) maps and grain orientation spread (GOS) maps characterizing grain orientation and DRX fraction: at 0.4 strain, DRX fraction is 38.9% with average grain size 81.50±6.32μm; at 0.5 strain, DRX fraction rises to 95.2% with average grain size 52.92±3.35μm; at 0.9 strain, DRX fraction reaches 97.7% with average grain size 50.71±3.29μm. Subfigure (b) shows 3D cellular automaton simulation results, with white as matrix, green as first-stage DRX grains, red as second-stage DRX grains. Simulated DRX fractions are 42.9%, 99.1%, 99.8% at 0.4, 0.5, 0.9 strain respectively, matching experimental trends well, validating the accuracy of the developed 3D CA model for multi-stage DRX prediction under varying deformation conditions.
